result.stdout:
Description:
  Excalibur database migration tool

Usage:
  excalibur-migrate [command] [options]

Options:
  -p, --provider <postgres|sqlserver> (REQUIRED)  Database provider (sqlserver, postgres)
  -c, --connection <connection> (REQUIRED)        Database connection string
  -a, --assembly <assembly>                       Assembly containing migration scripts (optional, uses entry assembly by default)
  -n, --namespace <namespace>                     Namespace prefix for migration resources (optional)
  -v, --verbose                                   Enable verbose output [default: False]
  --version                                       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help                                  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  up      Apply all pending migrations
  down    Roll back migrations to a specific version
  status  Show migration status (applied and pending)
  script  Generate SQL script for pending migrations (without applying)

commands.payload:
Description:
  Excalibur database migration tool

Usage:
  excalibur-migrate [command] [options]

Options:
  -p, --provider <postgres|sqlserver> (REQUIRED)  Database provider (sqlserver, postgres)
  -c, --connection <connection> (REQUIRED)        Database connection string
  -a, --assembly <assembly>                       Assembly containing migration scripts (optional, uses entry assembly by default)
  -n, --namespace <namespace>                     Namespace prefix for migration resources (optional)
  -v, --verbose                                   Enable verbose output [default: False]
  --version                                       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help                                  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  up      Apply all pending migrations
  down    Roll back migrations to a specific version
  status  Show migration status (applied and pending)
  script  Generate SQL script for pending migrations (without applying)

result.stdout:
Description:
  Roll back migrations to a specific version

Usage:
  excalibur-migrate down [options]

Options:
  -t, --to <to> (REQUIRED)                        Target migration ID to roll back to (this migration remains applied)
  -p, --provider <postgres|sqlserver> (REQUIRED)  Database provider (sqlserver, postgres)
  -c, --connection <connection> (REQUIRED)        Database connection string
  -a, --assembly <assembly>                       Assembly containing migration scripts (optional, uses entry assembly by default)
  -n, --namespace <namespace>                     Namespace prefix for migration resources (optional)
  -v, --verbose                                   Enable verbose output [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                                  Show help and usage information

  -a, --assembly <assembly>                       Assembly containing migration scripts (optional, uses entry assembly by default)

result.stdout:
Description:
  Generate SQL script for pending migrations (without applying)

Usage:
  excalibur-migrate script [options]

Options:
  -o, --output <output> (REQUIRED)                Output file path for the migration script
  -p, --provider <postgres|sqlserver> (REQUIRED)  Database provider (sqlserver, postgres)
  -c, --connection <connection> (REQUIRED)        Database connection string
  -a, --assembly <assembly>                       Assembly containing migration scripts (optional, uses entry assembly by default)
  -n, --namespace <namespace>                     Namespace prefix for migration resources (optional)
  -v, --verbose                                   Enable verbose output [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                                  Show help and usage information
